seoTitle: How to Use Mermaid Diagrams in Microsoft Loop
metaDescription: Add live Mermaid.js flowcharts and diagrams to Microsoft Loop pages using slash commands, and fix common preview rendering glitches.
intro: Instead of building process flows in a separate diagramming tool and re-uploading static images every time something changes, Microsoft Loop lets you write Mermaid.js code directly on the page and render it as a live diagram. This guide covers inserting a Mermaid block, writing basic diagram syntax, and troubleshooting preview issues.

steps.heading: Open the diagram block
steps.body: Place your cursor on a free line in your Loop page and type the /mermaid slash command to open the code entry module.

steps.heading: Write your Mermaid syntax
steps.body: Enter basic diagram logic such as flowchart TD for a top-down flowchart, or use sequence diagram syntax to map out a process.

steps.heading: Switch between code and preview views
steps.body: Use the header panel to toggle between Code view, Split-screen, and Live Preview so you can see your diagram render as you edit.

steps.heading: Fix grayed-out previews
steps.body: If the preview doesn't render, try clearing your browser cache, which resolves most stuck or grayed-out diagram display issues.

steps.heading: Share the live diagram
steps.body: Once your diagram looks right, Loop will auto-sync it across the shared workspace so teammates see the same up-to-date visual.

faq.question: Do I need to know how to code to use Mermaid in Loop?
faq.answer: You need to learn basic Mermaid syntax like flowchart TD or sequence diagrams, but it's much simpler than general programming and mostly involves naming nodes and connecting them with arrows.

faq.question: Can I make more than flowcharts with Mermaid in Loop?
faq.answer: Yes, Mermaid supports several diagram types including flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and Gantt charts, all written as text.

faq.question: Why is my Mermaid diagram stuck showing a blank or gray box?
faq.answer: This is usually a rendering glitch that can often be fixed by clearing your browser cache or refreshing the page.

faq.question: Can teammates edit the same Mermaid diagram together?
faq.answer: Yes, since it's a live code block on a shared Loop page, teammates can edit the underlying script and see updates reflected for everyone.
